reasoning: The Lean signature captures exactly the informal claim. `Odd p` together with `p.Prime` encodes "odd prime". Taking `a : (ZMod p)ˣ` is equivalent to a being coprime to p, which is the natural domain for the primitive root notion. `IsPrimitiveRoot (a : ZMod p) (p - 1)` is Mathlib's standard encoding of "a has order p-1 in (ZMod p)ˣ", i.e., a is a primitive root mod p. The RHS `∀ q, q ∣ (p-1) → q.Prime → ¬ a^((p-1)/q) = 1` is exactly "for every prime divisor q of p-1, a^((p-1)/q) ≠ 1 in ZMod p". The iff, quantifier structure, and types all match the informal statement.
